common.consent_message: We use cookies to measure traffic and improve Dragon App. You can accept or reject analytics & advertising cookies. See our Privacy Policy.
common.app_token_body: These apps are kept running mostly by AI. Instead of a coffee, you can chip in for the AI tokens that pay for ongoing maintenance.

index.title: Dragon App — Classic Mac apps, rebuilt
index.description: Dragon App is a small studio reviving beloved, discontinued macOS apps as fast, native, open-source rebuilds — ClipMenu 2, Yahoo! KeyKey 2, and Ice 2.
index.og_title: Dragon App — Classic Mac apps, rebuilt
index.og_description: Dragon App is a small studio reviving beloved, discontinued macOS apps as fast, native, open-source rebuilds — ClipMenu 2, Yahoo! KeyKey 2, and Ice 2.
index.hero_eyebrow: A small studio reviving classic Mac apps
index.hero_h1: Beloved Mac apps, rebuilt for today
index.hero_sub: Native, fast, and open source. Dragon App brings discontinued macOS classics back to life — one app at a time.
index.apps_sub: Each one is a faithful, modern rebuild — free and open source.
index.clipmenu_desc: Everything you copied, one shortcut away — a fast, native clipboard manager and history app.
index.keykey_desc: Yahoo! KeyKey (Yahoo!奇摩輸入法), the classic Cangjie (倉頡) & Simplex (速成) input method, rebuilt for modern macOS.

keykey.title: KeyKey — Yahoo!奇摩輸入法 Cangjie (倉頡) & Simplex (速成) for Mac
keykey.description: KeyKey is an open-source rebuild of the classic Yahoo! KeyKey (Yahoo!奇摩輸入法) Cangjie (倉頡) and Simplex (速成) input method for Mac — native, fast, and free. Download it now.
keykey.og_title: KeyKey — Yahoo!奇摩輸入法 Cangjie (倉頡) & Simplex (速成) for Mac
keykey.og_description: An open-source rebuild of the classic Yahoo! KeyKey Cangjie (倉頡) & Simplex (速成) input method for Mac — native, fast, and free.
keykey.ld_app_description: An open-source rebuild of the classic Yahoo! KeyKey (Yahoo!奇摩輸入法) Cangjie (倉頡) and Simplex (速成) Traditional-Chinese input method for Mac — native, fast, and free.
keykey.hero_h1: Yahoo! KeyKey, rebuilt for modern macOS
keykey.hero_sub: The classic Yahoo!奇摩輸入法 Cangjie (倉頡) and Simplex (速成) input method, reborn — native, fast, and open source.
keykey.hero_req: Requires macOS 12 or later · Apple Silicon · Signed & notarized · Open source
keykey.shot_aria: KeyKey Cangjie candidate window: typing the radicals 日 and 月 shows a numbered list of matching characters, with 明 selected first.
keykey.shot_menu_head: 輸入 日 月 — pick a candidate by number
keykey.features_h2: The Yahoo! KeyKey typing experience, brought back
keykey.features_sub: Fast, frictionless Cangjie and Simplex input that Mac users have missed — built fresh in Swift.
keykey.feat1_p: Both classic modes — full Cangjie (倉頡) and Simplex (速成) — with wildcard * matching when you don't remember every radical.
keykey.feat2_p: Candidates are ordered by how common they are, and the characters you pick rank higher over time — so your most-used words rise to the top.
keykey.feat3_p: After you commit a character, KeyKey suggests the words that usually follow — so common phrases come together in a single keystroke.
keykey.feat4_p: Toggle Traditional-to-Simplified output (輸出簡體字) and full-width punctuation (全形標點) right from the input menu — no separate preferences window.
keykey.feat5_p: A vertical candidate list matching the original layout — it follows the text caret and flips above or clamps to the screen so it's never cut off.
keykey.feat6_p: Shared resources keep memory use low across apps, and the full source is on GitHub — free, MIT licensed, and yours to inspect or build.
keykey.install_h2: Up and typing in four steps
keykey.install_sub: The installer is signed and notarized by Apple, so it opens cleanly — no Gatekeeper warnings.
keykey.step1_p: Grab YahooKeyKey2.pkg from the GitHub release and double-click it to install.
keykey.step2_p: It installs to ~/Library/Input Methods (no admin needed), then asks you to log out and back in.
keykey.step3_p: System Settings ▸ Keyboard ▸ Input Sources ▸ + ▸ Traditional Chinese → add 倉頡 and/or 速成.
keykey.step4_p: Press Ctrl-Space to switch to KeyKey and start typing. Toggle 簡體 or open 關於 from the input menu.
keykey.download_sub: Free and open source today, with a Mac App Store edition on the way.
keykey.plan1_li3: Full source on GitHub — inspect and build it yourself
keykey.plan1_li4: Both 倉頡 and 速成, with 繁→簡 output toggle
keykey.plan2_li2: Automatic updates through the App Store
keykey.plan2_li4: Not available yet — grab the GitHub build today
keykey.credit_body: KeyKey is an independent, open-source rebuild in Swift, created in tribute to the original Yahoo! KeyKey (Yahoo!奇摩輸入法) — the Traditional-Chinese input method many Mac users loved. It carries that experience forward for modern macOS. See the full credits; released under the MIT License.
keykey.footer_copyright: © 2026 Dragon App · KeyKey is open source under the MIT License.
keykey.faq1_q: Which input methods does KeyKey support?
keykey.faq1_a: KeyKey is a Traditional-Chinese input method with two modes: Cangjie (倉頡) and Simplex (速成, also called 簡易). It supports wildcard input with *, frequency-ranked candidates, associated phrases (聯想字詞), full-width punctuation, and an optional Traditional-to-Simplified output toggle.
keykey.faq2_a: Download the signed, notarized installer (YahooKeyKey2.pkg) from GitHub and double-click it. It installs to ~/Library/Input Methods without admin rights, then asks you to log out. After logging back in, add 倉頡 and/or 速成 under System Settings ▸ Keyboard ▸ Input Sources ▸ + ▸ Traditional Chinese, and switch with Ctrl-Space.
keykey.faq3_q: What do I need to run KeyKey?
keykey.faq3_a: A Mac with Apple Silicon (arm64) running macOS 12 Monterey or later. KeyKey is signed with a Developer ID and notarized by Apple, so it opens cleanly.
keykey.faq4_q: Is this the original Yahoo! KeyKey?
keykey.faq4_a: No. KeyKey is an independent, open-source rebuild in Swift, created in tribute to the original Yahoo! KeyKey (Yahoo!奇摩輸入法). It is released under the MIT License.

clipmenu.title: ClipMenu 2 — Clipboard Manager & History for Mac
clipmenu.description: ClipMenu 2 is a fast, native clipboard manager and history app for Mac. Everything you copied, one shortcut away — free on GitHub or the Mac App Store.
clipmenu.og_title: ClipMenu 2 — Clipboard Manager & History for Mac
clipmenu.og_description: Everything you copied, one shortcut away. A fast, native clipboard manager and history app for Mac.
clipmenu.ld_app_description: A fast, native clipboard manager and history app for Mac. Keep a history of everything you copy, save snippets in folders, and paste anywhere with a global hotkey.
clipmenu.hero_h1: Everything you copied, one shortcut away
clipmenu.hero_sub: A fast, native clipboard manager for Mac that remembers everything you copy, organizes your snippets, and pastes anywhere — with a single hotkey.
clipmenu.hero_req: Requires macOS 26 or later · Universal · Open source
clipmenu.shot_aria: ClipMenu 2 clipboard history popup showing recent copied items with numbers and an image thumbnail.
clipmenu.shot_menu_head: Recent · press ⌥V to open at the cursor
clipmenu.shot_clip5: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog
clipmenu.features_h2: Built for people who copy a lot
clipmenu.features_sub: Everything you need to move text and images around your Mac, and nothing you don't.
clipmenu.feat1_p: Every copy is remembered. Pop your recent clips right at the cursor or from the menu bar and paste in a keystroke.
clipmenu.feat2_p: Save the text you type all the time — replies, addresses, code — and organize it into folders you control.
clipmenu.feat3_p: Transform clips on the fly: change case, trim, encode HTML or Base64, hash, convert Japanese, and more.
clipmenu.feat4_p: Open the menu and paste straight into the frontmost app with shortcuts you assign. Your hands never leave the keyboard.
clipmenu.feat5_p: Copied an image? See a thumbnail inline, with numbered items and tooltips so you always grab the right one.
clipmenu.feat6_p: On the App Store edition, your snippets and settings sync across all your Macs through your own private iCloud.
clipmenu.how_sub: ClipMenu 2 lives quietly in your menu bar and gets out of your way.
clipmenu.how1_p: Keep working. ClipMenu 2 quietly keeps a history of everything you copy.
clipmenu.how2_p: The history pops up at your cursor. Pick a clip with the arrows or its number.
clipmenu.how3_p: ClipMenu 2 drops it into the app you're in — text, code, or an image.
clipmenu.locale_sub: ClipMenu 2 is fully localized in seven languages, right out of the box.
clipmenu.download_sub: Same app, your choice. Pick the one that fits how you work.
clipmenu.plan1_li1: Automatic updates through the App Store
clipmenu.plan1_li2: iCloud sync of snippets & settings across Macs
clipmenu.plan2_li3: Full source on GitHub — inspect and build it yourself
clipmenu.faq1_q: What's the difference between the App Store and GitHub versions?
clipmenu.faq1_a: They're the same app. The Mac App Store edition is a paid subscription that adds iCloud sync and updates automatically through the App Store. The GitHub edition is free and open source, with built-in auto-update, and includes every feature except iCloud sync.
clipmenu.faq2_q: Why does ClipMenu 2 ask for Accessibility permission?
clipmenu.faq2_a: macOS requires Accessibility permission for an app to paste into another app on your behalf. ClipMenu 2 uses it only to drop your selected clip into the frontmost app. Grant it under System Settings → Privacy & Security → Accessibility. (The App Store edition is sandboxed and doesn't use this.)
clipmenu.faq3_q: Where is my clipboard data stored?
clipmenu.faq3_a: On your Mac. Your history and snippets never leave your devices — ClipMenu 2 has no analytics, ads, or tracking, and we never receive your clipboard contents. With iCloud sync on, snippets travel through your own private iCloud account. Read the Privacy Policy.
clipmenu.faq4_a: The GitHub build is free forever. The Mac App Store edition is a 30-day free trial, then $9.99 per year, which covers iCloud sync and App Store updates. Manage or cancel anytime in your Apple Account → Subscriptions.
clipmenu.faq5_q: What do I need to run it?
clipmenu.faq5_a: A Mac running macOS 26 or later. ClipMenu 2 runs as a menu-bar agent with no Dock icon and stays out of your way.
clipmenu.faq6_a: ClipMenu 2 is an independent, modern rewrite in Swift, built on the legacy of the original ClipMenu by Naotaka Morimoto. We're grateful for his work, which is open source under the MIT License — ClipMenu 2 carries that spirit forward and is released under the same license.
clipmenu.ld_faq1_a: They're the same app. The Mac App Store edition is a paid subscription that adds iCloud sync and updates automatically through the App Store. The GitHub edition is free and open source, with built-in auto-update, and includes every feature except iCloud sync.
clipmenu.ld_faq2_a: macOS requires Accessibility permission for an app to paste into another app on your behalf. ClipMenu 2 uses it only to drop your selected clip into the frontmost app. The App Store edition is sandboxed and doesn't use this.
clipmenu.ld_faq3_a: On your Mac. Your history and snippets never leave your devices — ClipMenu 2 has no analytics, ads, or tracking, and we never receive your clipboard contents. With iCloud sync on, snippets travel through your own private iCloud account.
clipmenu.ld_faq4_a: The GitHub build is free forever. The Mac App Store edition is a 30-day free trial, then $9.99 per year, which covers iCloud sync and App Store updates. Manage or cancel anytime in your Apple Account.
clipmenu.ld_faq5_a: A Mac running macOS 26 or later. ClipMenu 2 runs as a menu-bar agent with no Dock icon and stays out of your way.
clipmenu.ld_faq6_a: ClipMenu 2 is an independent, modern rewrite in Swift, built on the legacy of the original ClipMenu by Naotaka Morimoto. It is open source under the MIT License.
clipmenu.final_sub: Give your clipboard a memory. It takes one minute to set up.
clipmenu.credit_body: ClipMenu 2 is built on the legacy of the original ClipMenu by Naotaka Morimoto. It is an independent, open-source rebuild in Swift — created with gratitude for his work and released under the same MIT License.
clipmenu.footer_copyright: © 2026 ClipMenu 2 · Open source under the MIT License. Original ClipMenu © Naotaka Morimoto.

support.title: ClipMenu 2 — Support & Help
support.description: Support and help for ClipMenu 2, the fast clipboard manager and history app for Mac. Setup, Accessibility permission, subscriptions, and FAQs.
support.og_description: Setup, Accessibility permission, subscriptions, and FAQs for ClipMenu 2, the clipboard manager for Mac.
support.tagline: The fast clipboard-history menu-bar app for macOS.
support.help_p1: The quickest way to ask a question, report a bug, or request a feature is on our public issue tracker. We read every report.
support.help_p2: Prefer email? Write to support@dragonapp.com and we'll get back to you.
support.faq1_q: How do I open my clipboard history?
support.faq1_a: Press your global hotkey (set in Settings) to pop the history right at your cursor, or click the ClipMenu 2 icon in the menu bar.
support.faq2_q: ClipMenu 2 can't paste into other apps.
support.faq2_a: macOS requires Accessibility permission to paste for you. Open System Settings → Privacy & Security → Accessibility and enable ClipMenu 2.
support.faq3_a: The Mac App Store edition is a paid app with a 30-day free trial, then an auto-renewing yearly subscription. It unlocks the full app and iCloud sync of your clipboard history and snippets across all your Macs.
support.faq4_q: How do I restore my subscription on a new Mac?
support.faq4_a: Open ClipMenu 2 and choose Restore Purchases on the welcome screen, signed in with the same Apple ID.
support.faq5_q: How do I manage or cancel the subscription?
support.faq5_a: Open the App Store app → click your name → Subscriptions, or visit your Apple subscriptions.
support.faq6_a: Your history and snippets stay on your Mac and in your own private iCloud database. We never receive them. See our Privacy Policy.
support.link_issues: Report a bug / request a feature
support.page_desc: Support and help for Dragon App's Mac apps — ClipMenu 2 (clipboard manager), Yahoo! KeyKey 2 (Cangjie & Simplex input method), and Ice 2 (menu bar manager). Setup, permissions, subscriptions, and FAQs.
support.page_tagline: Help for ClipMenu 2, Yahoo! KeyKey 2, and Ice 2.
support.cm_sub: The fast clipboard-history menu-bar app for macOS.
support.kk_sub: The Yahoo!奇摩輸入法 Cangjie (倉頡) & Simplex (速成) input method, rebuilt for macOS.
support.kk_help_p1: Questions, bug reports, and feature requests go to the KeyKey issue tracker. We read every one.
support.kk_faq1_a: Download YahooKeyKey2.pkg from the latest release and double-click it, or run brew install --cask teddychan/tap/yahoo-keykey-2. Then log out and back in.
support.kk_faq2_q: It doesn't show up in Input Sources.
support.kk_faq2_a: macOS only registers an input method at login. Log out and back in, then add it under System Settings → Keyboard → Input Sources → + → Traditional Chinese and choose 倉頡 (Cangjie) and/or 速成 (Simplex).
support.kk_faq3_q: How do I switch to KeyKey and change settings?
support.kk_faq3_a: Press Ctrl-Space to cycle input sources. From the input menu in the menu bar you can toggle 聯想字詞 (associated phrases), 全形標點 (full-width punctuation), and 輸出簡體字 (Simplified output), or open 關於 (About).
support.kk_faq4_q: How do I type Simplified Chinese?
support.kk_faq4_a: Turn on 輸出簡體字 in the input menu. You keep typing Cangjie/Simplex and KeyKey outputs Simplified characters.
support.kk_faq5_q: What do I need to run it?
support.kk_faq5_a: A Mac with Apple Silicon running macOS 12 or later. KeyKey is signed with a Developer ID and notarized by Apple, so it opens cleanly.
support.kk_faq6_a: Run brew uninstall --cask yahoo-keykey-2, or delete ~/Library/Input Methods/YahooKeyKey2.app. Then remove it from Input Sources and log out and back in.
support.ice_sub: The menu bar manager for macOS — hide, arrange, and reveal your menu bar items.
support.ice_help_p1: Questions, bug reports, and feature requests go to the Ice 2 issue tracker. We read every one.
support.ice_faq1_a: Download from the latest release and drag it to Applications, or run brew install --cask teddychan/tap/ice-2.
support.ice_faq2_q: How do I hide and reveal menu bar items?
support.ice_faq2_a: ⌘-drag the divider so the items to its left stay hidden. Click the Ice 2 icon, press your hotkey, or hover the edge to reveal them again.
support.ice_faq3_q: What do I need to run Ice 2?
support.ice_faq3_a: A Mac running macOS 26 or later.
support.ice_faq4_a: Run brew uninstall --cask ice-2, or quit Ice 2 and drag it from Applications to the Trash.

privacy.title: Dragon App — Privacy Policy
privacy.description: Privacy policy for Dragon App: how this website's consent-based analytics works, and how the ClipMenu 2, Yahoo! KeyKey 2, and Ice 2 Mac apps handle your data (they keep it on your device).

about.title: Why Dragon App — apps I love, kept alive
about.description: Dragon App revives macOS apps their original makers no longer maintain. The story behind the studio, and the promise behind every app.
about.story: Every app here is one I rely on every day — and that its original maintainer could no longer keep going. Rather than watch them break on each new macOS, I adopted them. Today they're maintained largely with the help of AI, which is also why support runs on 'Buy me Token' instead of coffee.
about.promise_macos_d: Runs on at least the latest two macOS releases.
about.promise_langs_d: Available in 7 languages, like this site.
about.promise_channels_d: Installable 3 ways: GitHub, Homebrew, and (where possible) the Mac App Store.
about.promise_icloud_d: Settings sync across your Macs via iCloud.

ice-2.title: Ice 2 — Menu Bar Manager for Mac, rebuilt for macOS 26 & 27
ice-2.description: Ice 2 is a free, open-source menu bar manager for Mac — hide and arrange menu bar items, then show them on demand. Rebuilt to support macOS 26 and 27.
ice-2.eyebrow: The menu bar manager for macOS
ice-2.h1: Tame your menu bar — back on modern macOS
ice-2.sub: Ice 2 hides the menu bar items you don't need, brings them back when you want them, and lets you lay the bar out your way. A faithful, open-source rebuild — now running on macOS 26 and 27.
ice-2.demo_caption: A Mac menu bar managed by Ice 2: rarely-used icons are tucked behind a divider, while the clock, battery, Wi-Fi and Control Center stay visible.
ice-2.feat1_p: Tuck rarely-used icons behind a divider so the bar stays clean.
ice-2.feat2_p: Move hidden items into a separate bar that appears on demand.
ice-2.feat3_p: Reveal items with a click, a hotkey, or by hovering the edge.
ice-2.support_body: I maintain Ice 2 for free in my spare time. Questions, bug reports and feature requests are genuinely welcome — open an issue on GitHub and I'll take a look.
ice-2.credit_body: Ice 2 is an independent, open-source rebuild of Ice, the menu bar manager originally created by Jordan Baird. This fork, actively maintained by Teddy Chan, carries it forward to modern macOS.

faq.a: Ice 2 hides the menu bar items you don't need so your menu bar stays clean, and brings them back on demand — with a click, a hotkey, or by hovering.
Download from the latest GitHub release and drag it to Applications, or install with Homebrew: brew install --cask teddychan/tap/ice-2.

faq.q: What do I need to run Ice 2?
faq.a: A Mac running macOS 26 or later.
It is an independent, open-source rebuild of Ice (originally by Jordan Baird), carried forward to modern macOS under GPLv3.

clipmenu-2.title: ClipMenu 2 — Clipboard Manager & History for Mac
clipmenu-2.description: ClipMenu 2 is a fast, native clipboard manager and history app for Mac. Everything you copied, one shortcut away — free on GitHub or the Mac App Store.
clipmenu-2.h1: Everything you copied, one shortcut away
clipmenu-2.sub: A fast, native clipboard manager for Mac that remembers everything you copy, organizes your snippets, and pastes anywhere — with a single hotkey.
clipmenu-2.demo_caption: ClipMenu 2 clipboard history popup showing recent copied items with numbers and an image thumbnail — press ⌥V to open it right at the cursor.
clipmenu-2.feat1_p: Every copy is remembered. Pop your recent clips right at the cursor or from the menu bar and paste in a keystroke.
clipmenu-2.feat2_p: Save the text you type all the time — replies, addresses, code — and organize it into folders you control.
clipmenu-2.feat3_p: Transform clips on the fly: change case, trim, encode HTML or Base64, hash, convert Japanese, and more.
clipmenu-2.support_body: Questions, bug reports, and feature requests are always welcome. Open an issue on GitHub and we'll get back to you — or write to support@dragonapp.com.
clipmenu-2.credit_body: ClipMenu 2 is built on the legacy of the original ClipMenu by Naotaka Morimoto. It is an independent, open-source rebuild in Swift — created with gratitude for his work and released under the same MIT License.

faq.q: What's the difference between the App Store and GitHub versions?
faq.a: They're the same app. The Mac App Store edition is a paid subscription that adds iCloud sync and updates automatically through the App Store. The GitHub edition is free and open source, with built-in auto-update, and includes every feature except iCloud sync.

faq.q: Why does ClipMenu 2 ask for Accessibility permission?
faq.a: macOS requires Accessibility permission for an app to paste into another app on your behalf. ClipMenu 2 uses it only to drop your selected clip into the frontmost app. Grant it under System Settings → Privacy & Security → Accessibility. (The App Store edition is sandboxed and doesn't use this.)

faq.q: Where is my clipboard data stored?
faq.a: On your Mac. Your history and snippets never leave your devices — ClipMenu 2 has no analytics, ads, or tracking, and we never receive your clipboard contents. With iCloud sync on, snippets travel through your own private iCloud account.
Download from the latest GitHub release and drag it to Applications, or install with Homebrew: brew install --cask teddychan/tap/clipmenu-2. The Mac App Store edition is also available.

yahoo-keykey-2.title: Yahoo KeyKey 2 — Yahoo!奇摩輸入法 Cangjie (倉頡) & Simplex (速成) for Mac
yahoo-keykey-2.description: Yahoo KeyKey 2 is an open-source rebuild of the classic Yahoo! KeyKey (Yahoo!奇摩輸入法) Cangjie (倉頡) and Simplex (速成) input method for Mac — native, fast, and free.
yahoo-keykey-2.eyebrow: The Cangjie & Simplex input method for macOS
yahoo-keykey-2.h1: Yahoo! KeyKey, rebuilt for modern macOS
yahoo-keykey-2.sub: The classic Yahoo!奇摩輸入法 Cangjie (倉頡) and Simplex (速成) input method, reborn — native, fast, and open source.
yahoo-keykey-2.demo_caption: Yahoo KeyKey 2 Cangjie candidate window: typing the radicals 日 and 月 shows a numbered list of matching characters, with 明 selected first.
yahoo-keykey-2.feat1_p: Both classic modes — full Cangjie (倉頡) and Simplex (速成) — with wildcard * matching when you don't remember every radical.
yahoo-keykey-2.feat2_p: Candidates are ordered by how common they are, and the characters you pick rank higher over time — so your most-used words rise to the top.
yahoo-keykey-2.feat3_p: After you commit a character, Yahoo KeyKey 2 suggests the words that usually follow — so common phrases come together in a single keystroke.
yahoo-keykey-2.support_body: Questions, bug reports, and feature requests are welcome — open an issue on GitHub and I'll take a look.
yahoo-keykey-2.credit_body: Yahoo KeyKey 2 is an independent, open-source rebuild in Swift, created in tribute to the original Yahoo! KeyKey (Yahoo!奇摩輸入法) — the Traditional-Chinese input method many Mac users loved. It carries that experience forward for modern macOS, released under the MIT License.

faq.q: Which input methods does Yahoo KeyKey 2 support?
faq.a: Yahoo KeyKey 2 is a Traditional-Chinese input method with two modes: Cangjie (倉頡) and Simplex (速成, also called 簡易). It supports wildcard input with *, frequency-ranked candidates, associated phrases (聯想字詞), full-width punctuation, and an optional Traditional-to-Simplified output toggle.

faq.q: How do I install Yahoo KeyKey 2?
faq.a: Download the signed, notarized installer (YahooKeyKey2.pkg) from GitHub and double-click it, or install with Homebrew: brew install --cask teddychan/tap/yahoo-keykey-2. Then log out and back in, and add 倉頡 and/or 速成 under System Settings ▸ Keyboard ▸ Input Sources ▸ + ▸ Traditional Chinese.

faq.q: What do I need to run Yahoo KeyKey 2?
faq.a: A Mac with Apple Silicon (arm64) running macOS 12 Monterey or later. Yahoo KeyKey 2 is signed with a Developer ID and notarized by Apple, so it opens cleanly.

faq.q: Is this the original Yahoo! KeyKey?
faq.a: No. Yahoo KeyKey 2 is an independent, open-source rebuild in Swift, created in tribute to the original Yahoo! KeyKey (Yahoo!奇摩輸入法). It is released under the MIT License.
